nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Account Settings
caption: Account Settings tab for a customer account in Ryuk. Shows top fields: Account ID (numeric internal ID), Email (primary login email), Currency, Subscription (e.g. Prepaid/Postpaid), Product Type (e.g. Voice+SMS), Enabled toggle (account active/inactive), Voice Enabled toggle, Zentrunk Enabled toggle. This is the main overview tab when you open any account. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search by email or Auth ID → click account → Account Settings tab.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Account Settings
caption: Account Settings tab scrolled to show billing and rate limits: Credits (current balance), Auth ID (the account's Auth ID / API key identifier used in all API calls), CPS Allowed (calls per second limit), Inbound CPS (inbound calls per second), Zentrunk CPS (CPS limit for Zentrunk SIP trunking), SMS MPS (SMS messages per second), Routing Profile (assigned routing profile name). Used to check CPS limits, verify Auth ID, see credit balance. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → Account Settings.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Account Settings
caption: Account Settings tab scrolled further showing: SMS Carrier Profile, SMS Routing, Voice Carrier Type (e.g. Plivo, BYOC), Plivo Carrier Fallback toggle (whether account falls back to Plivo carrier if primary fails), Last Login timestamp, Account Notes (internal notes field for support team). Used to check carrier configuration and read/write internal notes. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → Account Settings.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Account Settings
caption: Account Settings tab bottom section showing: Auto Recharge settings (threshold and top-up amount), Risk Status (risk classification of account), 2FA enabled/disabled, SMS and MMS specific settings. Used to check account risk level and auto-recharge configuration. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → Account Settings.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Customer Profile
caption: Customer Profile tab within an account in Ryuk. Shows customer's personal/business profile information separate from the technical account settings. Click the Customer Profile tab after opening an account to see this. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search by email/Auth ID → Customer Profile tab.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Sub Account Details
caption: Sub Account Details tab in Ryuk showing sub-accounts linked to the main account. Used to inspect sub-accounts (child accounts) under a parent account. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → Sub Account Details tab.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Additional Feature
caption: Additional Feature tab in Ryuk (first scroll). Shows additional feature flags and settings enabled for the account beyond standard Voice/SMS. Used to check what special features are provisioned for the customer. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → Additional Feature tab.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Additional Feature
caption: Additional Feature tab in Ryuk (second scroll). Continuation of special feature flags and configuration for the account. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → Additional Feature tab.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → WhatsApp Accounts
caption: WhatsApp Accounts tab in Ryuk showing WhatsApp Business API accounts linked to the customer. Used to look up WhatsApp channel configuration. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → WhatsApp Accounts tab.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → SMPP Accounts
caption: SMPP Accounts tab in Ryuk listing SMPP connections for the customer. Shows existing SMPP accounts with their credentials and status. Used to check if customer has SMPP configured. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → SMPP Accounts tab.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → SMPP Accounts → Add SMPP Account
caption: Add SMPP Account modal in Ryuk. Form to create a new SMPP connection for the customer. Click the Add button on the SMPP Accounts tab to open this. Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → SMPP Accounts tab → Add SMPP Account button.

nav_path: Account Management → Accounts → [search] → Carrier Relations Accounts
caption: Carrier Relations Accounts tab in Ryuk. Shows carrier relationship accounts associated with the customer (used for BYOC - Bring Your Own Carrier). Navigate: Account Management → Accounts → search → Carrier Relations Accounts tab.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline
caption: Voice Call Timeline search page in Ryuk. This is the main voice debugging hub. Has three tabs: Fetch CDRs (search call detail records), Voice Debugger (real-time debugging), Historical CDR Export (bulk CDR export). Enter a call UUID in the search box or use filters to pull up call details. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline.

nav_path: Voice → Recording Storage Management
caption: Recording Storage Management page in Ryuk. Used to manage voice call recordings stored by Plivo for the account. Can view, search, and manage recorded calls. Navigate: Voice → Recording Storage Management.

nav_path: Voice → PCAP Analyser v2
caption: PCAP Analyser v2 in Ryuk. Enter a call UUID to retrieve the PCAP (packet capture) file for that call. Used to analyse SIP signalling and RTP media at the packet level for deep audio quality troubleshooting. Navigate: Voice → PCAP Analyser v2 → enter UUID → fetch PCAP.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs
caption: Fetch CDRs page under Voice → Call Timeline in Ryuk. Search form includes: date range, from/to number, Account Auth ID, hangup cause, direction (inbound/outbound), call status. Results table shows CDRs with columns: date, from, to, direction, duration, hangup cause, status. Click a CDR row to see full CDR detail. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs tab → enter filters → Search.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail
caption: CDR Detail page in Ryuk (top section). Shows complete call details: Account Auth ID, Call UUID (unique identifier for the call), call direction (inbound/outbound), initiation time, answer time, end time, SIP Call Hangup cause, Call Status, Hangup cause code, From number, To number, carrier info. Also has Server Logs tab to see internal system logs. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → click a CDR row.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail
caption: CDR Detail page scrolled down showing additional call detail fields and the beginning of Server Logs section. Contains billing duration, rate, and other technical fields.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail → Server Logs
caption: Server Logs section within CDR Detail in Ryuk. Shows tabs for different internal system logs: PlivoCloud (application server logs), Outproxy (outbound SIP proxy logs), CDR Worker, MediaServer (media server/RTP logs), SIP Server. Used to trace exactly where a call failed by reading log entries from each component. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → click CDR → Server Logs section.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail → Server Logs → MediaServer
caption: MediaServer logs tab in CDR Detail Server Logs. Shows RTP media server log entries for the call. Used to diagnose audio quality issues (one-way audio, no audio, echo) by examining what the media server processed. Navigate: Voice → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail → Server Logs → MediaServer tab.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail → Server Logs → MediaServer
caption: MediaServer logs tab (continued scroll) in CDR Detail. More RTP media server log entries for diagnosing audio issues.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail → Customer Debug Logs
caption: Customer Debug Logs tab within CDR Detail in Ryuk. Shows application-level debug logs from the customer's application server (callbacks, XML responses, errors). Used to see what the customer's IVR/application returned and identify XML errors or callback failures. Navigate: Voice → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail → Customer Debug Logs tab.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Timeline v2
caption: CDR Insights page in Ryuk, Call Timeline v2 tab. Accessed by clicking a call UUID in Call Timeline results. Shows a visual timeline of the call with three sub-tabs: Call Progression (detailed event log), Call Timeline (visual), Call Relations (related calls). Top section shows: Call UUID, account Auth ID, from/to numbers, call direction, start/end time, duration, hangup cause. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → search → click a call UUID → CDR Insights.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Relations & Stats
caption: CDR Insights page showing Call Relations section (related calls linked to same session/conference) and Call Stats section (summary statistics). Used to find parent/child calls and see aggregate call metrics. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → click UUID → CDR Insights.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Audio Metrics
caption: CDR Insights audio quality metrics in Ryuk. Shows: Jitter (ms), Packet Loss (%), MOS score (Mean Opinion Score, 1-5 scale where 4+ is good, 3.5 is acceptable, below 3 is poor quality). MOS score is the single most important audio quality indicator. Also shows Call Timeline visual with audio quality overlaid. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → click UUID → CDR Insights → scroll to audio section.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression
caption: Call Progression tab in CDR Insights showing a colour-coded visual timeline of the call. Each segment of the timeline bar represents a different call state/event (ringing, connected, on hold, etc.) with colour coding. Helps visualise where time was spent in a call. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → click UUID → CDR Insights → Call Progression tab.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs
caption: Call Progression Logs table in Ryuk. Shows a chronological event log for the call with columns: Time, Event Type (Xml Event / Callback Event / Call State Event), Event name (Startapp, Speak, Redirect, GetDigits, Record, Dial, Hangup, etc.), Status (200 OK, 408, errors), Issues (warnings like High Latency). Each row can be expanded to see the full payload. This is the primary tool for debugging IVR call flow issues. Navigate: Voice → Call Timeline → click UUID → CDR Insights → Call Progression tab → Logs section.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs → Startapp event
caption: Startapp callback event expanded in Call Progression Logs. Shows the POST request payload sent to the customer's application URL when a call starts. Contains: callUUID, from, to, callStatus, direction, and all call parameters. Used to verify the initial webhook was sent and what data the customer's app received. Click the expand arrow on a Startapp row in the logs table.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs → Speak XML event
caption: Speak XML event expanded in Call Progression Logs. Shows the XML response from the customer's application containing a <Speak> verb (text-to-speech). Visible fields include the text being spoken, voice, language. Used to verify what TTS was played to the caller. Expand the Speak row in the logs.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs → Redirect XML event
caption: Redirect XML event expanded in Call Progression Logs. Shows a <Redirect> verb in the customer's XML response, which transfers call control to a new URL. Contains the redirect URL. Used to trace call flow redirections between different application endpoints.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs → Redirect 408 error
caption: Redirect Callback Event showing a 408 Request Timeout error in Call Progression Logs. The Issues column shows a 408 status indicating the customer's callback URL did not respond in time. This causes call failures when the application server is slow or unreachable. Used to identify timeout issues in customer's IVR.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs → XML Validation Error
caption: XML Validation Error and Call Hangup events in Call Progression Logs. Shows when Plivo rejected the customer's XML response due to invalid XML syntax or unsupported verbs. The call then hangs up. Used to diagnose XML parsing errors in IVR applications.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs → Hangup callback
caption: Final Hangup Callback event expanded in Call Progression Logs. Shows the POST payload sent to the customer's hangup URL, containing: callUUID, callStatus (completed/failed/busy/no-answer), hangupCause, duration, billingDuration, from, to, direction, and all final call parameters. This is the last event in any call and contains the definitive hangup reason.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression
caption: Call Progression timeline for a second example call in Ryuk. Shows the visual timeline bar with events including Startapp, Getdigits (DTMF collection), Call Answered, Redirect, Record, Dial (outbound leg), Call Hangup with WARNING High Latency, Recordstop. This is a typical IVR call flow with recording and dial-out.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Logs
caption: Logs table for second example call showing events: Startapp, Getdigits (DTMF collection), Call Answered, Redirect, Record (call recording started), Dial, Call Hangup, Hangup with WARNING High Latency indicator, Recordstop. High Latency warning appears in the Issues column when callback response time exceeded threshold.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Startapp payload
caption: Startapp POST payload expanded (scroll 1). Shows full webhook parameters including: from, to, callUUID, direction, callStatus, ANI, DNIS, SIP headers, authentication token, session ID. The token field is used for secure callback validation.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Startapp payload
caption: Startapp POST payload (scroll 2). Additional parameters including billing details, account information, and full token/authentication data sent in the startapp callback.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Startapp payload
caption: Startapp POST payload (scroll 3). Final parameters in the startapp callback payload.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → GetDigits XML event
caption: GetDigits XML event expanded in Call Progression Logs (scroll 1). Shows the <GetDigits> verb XML with parameters: finishOnKey (#, *, etc.), validDigits (accepted DTMF tones), timeout (seconds to wait), numDigits (max digits to collect). GetDigits is used to collect DTMF input from callers in IVR menus.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → GetDigits XML event
caption: GetDigits XML event (scroll 2). Additional GetDigits parameters including the action URL where digits will be posted, method, and nested Speak element inside GetDigits for prompting the caller.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Redirect with Record response
caption: Redirect Callback Event showing a 200 OK response containing a Record XML verb. The customer's application responded to a redirect with instructions to start recording the call. Shows the Record URL, callback URL, and recording parameters.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Record XML event
caption: Record XML event expanded in Call Progression Logs. Shows the <Record> verb with parameters: finishOnKey, recordChannelType (stereo/mono), startOnDialAnswer, redirect, timeout, recordSession, method (POST), callbackMethod, transcriptionMethod, playBeep. Used to verify recording configuration.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Dial XML event
caption: Dial XML event expanded in Call Progression Logs. Shows the <Dial> verb parameters: redirect, callerId, callerName, method (POST), callbackMethod, transcriptionMethod. The Dial verb is used to bridge an inbound call to an outbound leg (e.g. agent phone). Used to diagnose why outbound legs are failing.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Dial callback
caption: Dial Callback Event showing the application's response to a dial callback. Contains SIP headers, X-Channel, X-Duration, X-CallType. The callback was to a sales agent action URL. Shows the application acknowledged the dial event and provided redirect instructions.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Hangup High Latency
caption: Hangup Callback Event with WARNING: High Latency in Call Progression Logs. The Issues column shows WARNING High Latency — this means the customer's hangup callback URL responded slowly (high latency). Shows full hangup payload including: callStatus, hangupCause (NORMAL_CLEARING), dialBLegStatus, dialBLegDuration, billingDuration, totalCost. High Latency warning is triggered when callback response exceeds ~3 seconds.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → Hangup High Latency
caption: Hangup Callback Event WARNING High Latency (scroll 2). Continued payload showing full hangup response including latency value (14568ms in example), billingDuration, callStatus. Latency of 14 seconds indicates very slow customer application response.

nav_path: Voice → Call Timeline → CDR Insights → Call Progression → RecordStop callback
caption: RecordStop Callback Event in Call Progression Logs. Sent to the customer's callback URL when a recording is stopped. Contains: recordingURL (where the recording file can be downloaded), recordingDuration (seconds), callerName, callerNumber, recordingStartMs, recordingEndMs, callUUID. Used to verify recording was captured and get the recording URL.

nav_path: Voice → Voice Geo Permissions → Country Permissions
caption: Voice Geo Permissions page in Ryuk (Country Permissions tab). Search by Username to see and modify which countries an account can make outbound calls to. Table shows: Country, Continent, Source Of Update (console/admin), Can Override From Console toggle, Custom Block checkbox. Enabled = outbound calls allowed to that country. This is the page to enable or block specific countries for voice calls. Navigate: Voice → Voice Geo Permissions → enter username → Country Permissions tab.

nav_path: Voice → Voice Geo Permissions → Country Permissions
caption: Voice Geo Permissions Country Permissions (scroll 2). More countries listed with their enabled/blocked status. Canada shown as Can Override From Console enabled. Actions: Select All Countries, Deselect All Countries, Hard Disable, Revert Hard Disable, Save Changes.

nav_path: Voice → Voice Geo Permissions → Country Permissions
caption: Voice Geo Permissions Country Permissions (scroll 3). More countries including Dominican Republic (enabled), Algeria, Ecuador, Estonia. Shows how individual countries are toggled on/off.

nav_path: Voice → Voice Geo Permissions → Country Permissions
caption: Voice Geo Permissions Country Permissions (scroll 4). Countries including Puerto Rico (Can Override From Console enabled, toggle on), Palestine. Custom Block checkbox available per country.

nav_path: Voice → Voice Geo Permissions → Country Permissions
caption: Voice Geo Permissions Country Permissions (scroll 5). Countries including Ukraine, Uganda, U.S. Minor Outlying Islands (Custom Block checked), United States (Can Override enabled, Custom Block checked). Shows US has special flags set.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Trunks → Outbound
caption: Zentrunk Trunks page, Outbound tab in Ryuk. Search for outbound trunks by Trunk Domain or Auth ID. Results show: Trunk name, Domain, IP Group (click to see allowed IPs), Auth Group, Trunk Status (enabled/disabled), Trunk CPS. Example: CRSSTALK-Outbound trunk with domain 3061612387502241.zt.plivo.com. Used to verify Zentrunk outbound trunk configuration. Navigate: Zentrunk → Trunks → Outbound tab.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Trunks → Outbound → IP Group
caption: IP Group modal in Zentrunk Outbound Trunks. Click the IP Group link to see the IP addresses/CIDR ranges allowed to send SIP traffic on this trunk. Example: 3CX_SIL group shows 138.89.151.24/32. Used to verify or troubleshoot IP whitelisting for Zentrunk.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Trunks → Inbound
caption: Zentrunk Trunks page, Inbound tab in Ryuk. Search for inbound trunks by phone number or Auth ID. Results show: Trunk name, Numbers (DIDs assigned to trunk), Primary URI (SIP destination for inbound calls), Fallback URI, Trunk Status. Example: CRSSTALK-INBOUND trunk with numbers 17328101680 and 17324407338, Primary URI SIL-3CX. Navigate: Zentrunk → Trunks → Inbound tab.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Trunks → Inbound → URI Detail
caption: SIP URI detail modal in Zentrunk Inbound Trunks. Click the URI link to see the full SIP URI configuration. Example: SIL-3CX shows URI: 138.89.151.24:5061;transport=tls and Auth Enabled: False. Used to verify destination SIP URI and TLS/auth settings for inbound Zentrunk calls. Navigate: Zentrunk → Trunks → Inbound → click URI link.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Trunks → Inbound → URI Detail
caption: SIP URI detail modal in Zentrunk Inbound Trunks (additional view). Shows same URI configuration: URI, Auth Enabled status.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs
caption: Zentrunk Fetch CDRs page in Ryuk. Two tabs: CDR (search) and Call Insights. Search form: Zentrunk Call UUID, Voice Call UUID, From Number, To Number, Date range, Hangup Cause, Direction (inbound/outbound), From/To Country, Carrier ID/Name, Domestic Call filter. Results table: Date, From, To, Account ID, Direction, To Country, Hangup Cause, Duration, Call Insights link (Generate button), STIR Verification, STIR Attestation. Navigate: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs
caption: Zentrunk Fetch CDRs results page (second view/scroll). Shows the CDR list with all columns visible.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail
caption: Zentrunk CDR Detail modal (scroll 1). Click a CDR row to see full details. Fields: Call UUID (Zentrunk), Call ID (Voice Call UUID linking to Voice CDR), To Number (with carrier/type), From Number, Initiation Time, Answer Time, End Time, Duration, Bill Duration, Call Direction, Destination Network Group, Origin Network Group, Hangup Cause, Call Rate, Total Amount (cost), Originator (customer/carrier), Hangup Code, Cause Description. Navigate: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → click a row.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → CDR Detail
caption: Zentrunk CDR Detail (scroll 2). Additional fields: STIR Verification (Verified/Not Verified), Carrier Verstat, Stir Attestation (A/B/C), Product (Zentrunk), Domestic Call (Domestic/Non Domestic), SIP PCAP links (Customer Facing and Internal Facing). The SIP PCAP links let you download the actual packet capture for deep debugging.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → Call Insights
caption: Zentrunk Call Insights page (Call Insights tab). Access by clicking Call Insights / Generate button on a CDR row. Shows: Call Details section (Initiation Time, End Time, From, To, Direction, Call Duration, Total Cost, Call State, Domestic Call), Call Statistics section (Hangup Source, SRTP Enabled, Zentrunk Media IP, Carrier Media IP, Trunk ID, Post Dial Delay, RTP Ports Customer-to-Zentrunk, Signalling IP, Signalling Protocol). Buttons: Download PCAP, Overall SIP Flow. Navigate: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → click Generate in Call Insights column.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → Call Insights → SIP Flow Customer Leg
caption: Zentrunk Call Insights SIP Flow - Customer Leg diagram. Shows a ladder/sequence diagram of SIP messages between Customer, Loadbalancer, and Appserver. Messages: INVITE, 100 Trying, 180 Ringing, 200 OK, ACK, BYE, 200 OK. Used to verify the SIP signalling flow on the customer side. Also shows Audio Quality Statistics: Overall Zentrunk Call Quality Score (e.g. 4.3/5), MOS for A leg, MOS for B leg.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → Call Insights → Audio Quality
caption: Zentrunk Call Insights audio quality and media statistics. Shows: Audio Quality Statistics (Overall Zentrunk Call Quality Score, MOS for A leg, MOS for B leg - all out of 5), Media - Carrier Leg (CODEC Used), Media - SIP Trunk Leg (CODEC Used), Audio Call Statistics (Hangup Cause: normal_hangup, Hangup Code: 3010, Jitter micro sec, Round Trip Time micro sec). Used to diagnose codec mismatches and audio quality for Zentrunk calls.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → Call Insights
caption: Zentrunk Call Insights page (Call Details section). Duplicate view showing call detail fields for reference.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → Call Insights → Overall SIP Flow
caption: Zentrunk Overall SIP Flow diagram (scroll 1). Accessed by clicking 'Overall SIP Flow' button on Call Insights. Shows complete 5-leg SIP ladder: Customer → Loadbalancer → Appserver → OutboundSBC → Carrier. Full chronological SIP message exchange across all nodes with timestamps. Used for deep SIP troubleshooting when a Zentrunk call fails to connect.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Fetch CDRs → Call Insights → Overall SIP Flow
caption: Zentrunk Overall SIP Flow diagram (scroll 2). Continuation showing BYE messages and final handshake in the 5-leg SIP sequence.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Geo Permissions → Country Permissions
caption: Zentrunk Geo Permissions page in Ryuk. Three tabs: VNG Risk Status, Account Permissions, Premium Prefixes. Choose Account (Auth ID) and Choose Outbound Trunk to view permissions. Country Permissions tab shows 245 countries with: Country, Continent, Source of Update, Can Override From Console toggle, Custom Block checkbox. All countries shown as 'Enabled' by default (Africa countries: Algeria, Angola, Benin, Botswana, etc.). Navigate: Zentrunk → Geo Permissions → select account and trunk.

nav_path: Zentrunk → Geo Permissions → Country Permissions
caption: Zentrunk Geo Permissions Country Permissions (scroll 2). European countries shown: Moldova, Monaco, Montenegro, Netherlands, Norway, Poland (Custom Block checked), Portugal, Romania, Russia.

nav_path: PHLO → PHLO List
caption: PHLO List page in Ryuk. Search for PHLOs (visual call flow builder applications) by user email. Results show: PHLO Name, PHLO ID (UUID), Actions (Copy ID, Copy URL, View Logs). Green dot = active PHLO. Used to find a customer's PHLO flows and get their IDs or view execution logs. Navigate: PHLO → PHLO List → enter customer email → Search.

nav_path: PHLO → PHLO List
caption: PHLO List showing multiple PHLOs for a customer. Example: ar@haulmont.com has 5 PHLOs (US Flow, UK Support new flow, Australia Support, A&B Support, UK Support2, UK Support). Shows how customers can have multiple named PHLOs for different use cases. Green dots indicate all PHLOs are active.

nav_path: PHLO → PHLO Logs
caption: PHLO Logs page in Ryuk. Shows execution log runs for a specific PHLO. Filters: email, PHLO Name, Status, Any Trigger. Results table: PHLO Run ID (UUID), PHLO Name, Trigger Type (Incoming Call), Status (Success/Failed), From number, To number. Used to debug PHLO execution failures. Navigate: PHLO → PHLO Logs → enter email → Search.

nav_path: Short Code → Short Code Verification
caption: Short Code Verification page in Ryuk. Lists all registered short codes with expand/edit/delete actions. Short codes shown are 5-6 digit codes used for SMS campaigns. Each entry can be expanded to see the Join, Help, and Stop messages configured. Navigate: Short Code → Short Code Verification.

nav_path: Short Code → Short Code Rentals
caption: Short Code Rentals page in Ryuk. Shows accounts that have rented short codes. Columns: Account ID, Username, Amount (monthly fee), Shortcode (the code), Previous Charge Date, Charge Interval (monthly), Enabled, Reason (internal notes). Used to check short code rental billing status. Navigate: Short Code → Short Code Rentals.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Phone Number Inventory
caption: Phone Number Inventory search page in Ryuk. Search Plivo's global DID inventory by: Phone Number (exact match), Number Type (Local/TollFree), Carrier, Carrier Tier, Added On/Before dates, Region, Country, Rental Status (rented/available), Status, Rate Center, Features (capabilities), Lata. Navigate: Numbers → Number Management → Phone Number Inventory tab.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Phone Number Inventory
caption: Phone Number Inventory search results. Columns: Number, Type (Local/TollFree), Region, Carrier, Tier, Added On date, Rented (Y/N), Blocked (Y/N), Voice enabled, SMS enabled, MMS enabled, Prerequisite. Example: 17328101680 is Local, New Jersey, Level3/Syniverse, Level3-L1/Syniverse US Local tier, added Jan 2020, rented, not blocked, Voice Y, SMS Y, MMS Y.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Account Phone Numbers
caption: Account Phone Numbers search page in Ryuk. Search for numbers assigned to a specific account. Filters: Auth ID, Subaccount, Application, Number (exact), Number Type, Region, Carrier ID/Name, Capabilities, Date Added On, Active/Blocked/Any radio, Plivo Number/Non-Plivo Number/Any radio. Used to see all numbers on a customer's account. Navigate: Numbers → Number Management → Account Phone Numbers tab.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Account Phone Numbers → Capabilities
caption: Capabilities filter dropdown on Account Phone Numbers search. Options: Voice Enabled, Voice Disabled, SMS Enabled, SMS Disabled, MMS Enabled, MMS Disabled. Use this to filter numbers by their enabled capabilities.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Account Phone Numbers
caption: Account Phone Numbers search results showing numbers assigned to account. Columns: Number, Account (Auth ID), Sub Account, Application, Added On date, SMS Enabled, MMS Enabled, Voice Enabled, Plivo Number, Active, Number Type, Region, Country, (more columns scrolled right). Actions available: Unrent, Edit, Activate, Export as CSV, Add to Powerpack. Example shows 2 numbers linked to CRSSTALK-INBOUND application.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Account Phone Numbers
caption: Account Phone Numbers results scrolled right. Additional columns: Carrier, Tier, Address ID, Identity ID, Actions (Link Verification), Toll-Free Verification status, Toll-Free verification request status, Renewal Date, CNAM Lookup.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Account Phone Numbers
caption: Account Phone Numbers results (far right columns). Shows Verification status (for toll-free compliance), Toll-Free verification request status, Renewal Date, CNAM Lookup enabled/disabled.

nav_path: Numbers → Number Management → Phone Number Inventory Logs
caption: Phone Number Inventory Logs page in Ryuk. Shows history of number rentals across all accounts. Columns: Number, Account (Auth ID and username), Rented (checkmark), Rented On date, Rented To date. Filter by number or username/email. Total 1,675,045 records. Used to track number rental history across platform. Navigate: Numbers → Number Management → Phone Number Inventory Logs tab.

nav_path: Numbers → Compliance → Compliance Requirements
caption: Compliance Requirements page in Ryuk, filtered for India. Shows regulatory document requirements for purchasing numbers in India. Fields: Compliance Information (country, number type, end user type), Version Info, Created Between. Results: ID, Country, Number Type, End User Type, Description, Scope, Allowed Document Types, Version. Example: India Local Business requires Proof of Incorporation and GST Certificate (National scope). Navigate: Numbers → Compliance → Compliance Requirements → select country.

nav_path: Numbers → Compliance → Compliance Requirements
caption: Compliance Requirements page for United States. Shows 0 records — no special compliance documents required to purchase US numbers. Navigate: Numbers → Compliance → Compliance Requirements → select United States.

nav_path: Numbers → Compliance → Compliance Requirements
caption: Compliance Requirements for UAE (United Arab Emirates). 5 records showing tollfree number requirements: Business users need Letter of Intent, Business Registration Document, and Business Address (all Foreign scope). Individual users need End User Address Details and Proof of Address. Navigate: Numbers → Compliance → Compliance Requirements → select United Arab Emirates.

nav_path: Numbers → Porting → Port In
caption: Port In page in Ryuk. Search for number porting requests by: Port Order ID/Name, Auth ID/Customer Email, Phone Number, FOC date range, Created Date range, Port Order Status, Phone Number Port Status. Results show 2176 total records with columns: Phone Number, Email, Port Order Name, Port Order Status, Phone Number Port Status, FOC Date, Created Date. Navigate: Numbers → Porting → Port In.

nav_path: Numbers → Number History
caption: Number History page in Ryuk. Enter a phone number to see its complete history. Shows Number Information section (Type, Region, Carrier, Tier, Added On, Rented, Blocked, Voice, SMS flags) and History section with event log: Time, Event, Details. Events include: SMS/Voice Provisioning Done, Number Last Rented (with customer name), testing events. Used to track who had a number previously and what changes were made. Navigate: Numbers → Number History → enter number → Search.

nav_path: Numbers → Incoming Number Rate → Profiles
caption: Incoming Number Rate - Customer Profile Profiles list in Ryuk. Shows all risk profiles and how many accounts are assigned to each. Profiles: Block All (232,295 accounts), Medium_Risk (74,164), Safe_Regular (18,927), Low_Risk (11,208), High_Risk (9,505), High_Risk_Temp (7,312), Safe_Regular_IN (4,771), Medium_Risk_Temp (2,480), Low_Risk_Temp (1,893), Safe_Regular_Temp (1,417). Also has Account Mapping tab. Navigate: Numbers → Incoming Number Rate → Profiles tab.

nav_path: Numbers → Incoming Number Rate → Account Mapping
caption: Incoming Number Rate Account Mapping page. Search by Auth ID to see which risk profile an account is assigned to. Can also add new profile mapping. Example: Account 10045544 (jrapczynski@aciedge.com) is mapped to Safe_Regular profile. Navigate: Numbers → Incoming Number Rate → Account Mapping tab → enter Auth ID → Search.

nav_path: Finance → Invoices → Invoice Search
caption: Finance Invoices page in Ryuk. Invoice Search tab. Multiple tabs: Invoice Search, USD Invoice, Currency Invoice, Generate Invoice, Generate INR Invoice, Generate Disabled Accounts Invoice, Commit Taxes, Assign Currency, Negative Balance Accounts. Search form: account type checkboxes (Developer, Prepaid, PostPaid, Internal), Account search field, From Month/Year, To Month/Year. Navigate: Finance → Invoices → Invoice Search tab.

nav_path: Finance → Invoices → Invoice Search
caption: Finance Invoice Search results. Columns: Username (Auth ID and type), Invoice Details (invoice ID link), Console Exports (Usage Report link), Invoice Date, Duration, Usage Charges, Recurring Charges, Shortcode Rentals, Total Spend, Minimum Commit Shortfall, Minimum Monthly Commit. Actions: Select all, Export as CSV, Download Invoice PDFs, Bulk Approve and Send Invoices, Mark Invoice as Failed.

nav_path: Finance → Invoices → Invoice Search
caption: Finance Invoice Search results (scroll 2). Shows two invoices for same account across two months: Feb 2026 (Usage 0.43, Recurring 1.00, Total 1.43) and Jan 2026 (Usage 5.77, Recurring 1.12, Total 6.89). Each has a Usage Report link for detailed breakdown.

nav_path: Finance → Invoices → Invoice Search
caption: Finance Invoice Search results (scroll 3, right side columns). Additional columns: Discount, Pending Balance, Credited Payments, Adjustments, Late Payment Fee, GST, Tax, CCRF, One-time Charges, Carrier Surcharge Fees, CCPF, Invoice Amount, Action (Approve button). Approve button sends invoice to customer.

nav_path: Finance → Invoices → Usage Report → Zentrunk Inbound sheet
caption: Usage Details spreadsheet (Numbers/Excel) for a Plivo account showing Zentrunk Inbound usage. Tab: Zentrunk Inbound. Columns: Country, Type (Local), Capability (Voice SMS MMS), Number of Calls, Minutes, Charges. Example: United States, Local, Voice+SMS+MMS, 2 calls, 15 min, $0.05 and 96 calls, 512 min, $1.54. Subtotal: $1.58. Downloaded from Usage Report link in invoice.

nav_path: Finance → Invoices → Usage Report → Zentrunk Outbound sheet
caption: Usage Details spreadsheet showing Zentrunk Outbound usage. Tab: Zentrunk Outbound. Columns: Country, Number of Calls, Minutes, Charges. Example: United States, 44 calls, 169 minutes, $4.31. Subtotal: $4.31. Part of the detailed usage report downloaded from an invoice.

nav_path: Finance → Invoices → Usage Report → Other Charges sheet
caption: Usage Details spreadsheet Other Charges tab. Shows: State and Local Taxes ($0.38), Carrier Cost Recovery Charge ($0.15), USF Contribution ($0.24), Subtotal $0.77. Notes explain: voice agent call time rounded to pulse blocks (15s, 30s, 60s), converted to minutes, charged at per-minute rate.

nav_path: Finance → Recurring Charge
caption: Recurring Charge list page in Ryuk. Shows all recurring charges across all accounts. Columns: Account, Charge Type (Support Plan Priority/Business/Personalized, Additional MPS, Additional Voice API CPS, Additional Zentrunk CPS, Sender ID, Others), Recurring Charge Type (fixed/percentage), Amount/Min, Charge Percentage, Charge Line Items, Next Charge Date, Previous Charge Date, Charge Amount, Charge Interval (month), Charge From date, Charge Upto date, Enabled, Invoice Comment. Filter by account or charge type. Add Recurring Charge button. Navigate: Finance → Recurring Charge.

nav_path: Finance → Recurring Charge → Fair Usage Surcharge
caption: Fair Usage Surcharge tab in Recurring Charge. Shows surcharge rules applied to accounts that violate fair usage policy. Surcharge types: Abandoned Calls (enabled, Non-SIP, ALL except India, 20% threshold, $0.005/call), Short Duration Calls (enabled, Non-SIP, ALL except India, 10%, $0.015/call), Average Call Duration (disabled, Non-SIP, US only), Incomplete Calls to TF (disabled, Non-SIP, US, 5%, $0.01/call). Note: to update exclusion list contact fin-systems@plivo.com. Navigate: Finance → Recurring Charge → Fair Usage Surcharge tab.

nav_path: Finance → Recurring Charge → Add Recurring Charge
caption: Add Recurring Charge modal in Ryuk. Fields: Account (enter Auth ID/username), Charge Type (dropdown), Charge From (Charge Immediately checkbox + choose invoice month/year), Charge To (Charge Indefinitely checkbox + choose invoice month/year). Click Proceed to confirm. Used by billing team to add monthly recurring charges for support plans, additional CPS, MPS upgrades. Navigate: Finance → Recurring Charge → Add Recurring Charge button.

nav_path: Finance → Recurring Charge → Add Recurring Charge → Charge Type
caption: Add Recurring Charge modal with Charge Type dropdown expanded (scroll 1). Available charge types: Additional Voice API CPS Charges, Additional Zentrunk CPS Charges, Additional MPS Charges, Sender ID Charges, Support Plan - Priority, Support Plan - Business, Support Plan - Personalized, Support Plan - Developer.

nav_path: Finance → Recurring Charge → Add Recurring Charge → Charge Type
caption: Add Recurring Charge modal Charge Type dropdown (scroll 2). More options: Additional MPS Charges, Sender ID Charges, Support Plan - Priority, Support Plan - Business, Support Plan - Personalized, Support Plan - Developer, Others.

nav_path: Finance → One-time Charges
caption: One-time Charges page in Ryuk. Lists all one-time charges applied to accounts. Search by Account, Charge Category, State, Invoice Period. Total 45,436 records. Columns: Account, Amount, Charge Category (Others), State (enabled), Invoice Period (03-2026), Invoice Line Item (One time Short code setup fee, Shortcode Setup Fee, Short Code Setup Fee, Minimum Commit Shortfall for January 2026, BMTHKZJ Brand resubmission, Toll-free setup charge for 5 numbers). Navigate: Finance → One-time Charges.

nav_path: Finance → One-time Charges → Add One-Time Charge
caption: Add One-Time Charge modal in Ryuk. Fields: Account (enter Auth ID), Amount, Charge Category (dropdown), Customer Facing Invoice Line Item (text shown to customer on invoice), Invoice Period (month/year), Additional Comments (internal notes, mention support ticket ID or carrier name for sender ID charges). Click Add One-Time Charge to save. Navigate: Finance → One-time Charges → Add One-Time Charge button.

nav_path: Finance → One-time Charges → Add One-Time Charge → Charge Category
caption: Add One-Time Charge Charge Category dropdown. Options: Average Call Duration Surcharge, Abandoned Call Surcharge, Short Duration Call Surcharge, Incomplete calls to Incoming Toll-Free Call Surcharge, Sender ID Setup, Others.
